items.title: Legal action filed over decision to clear almost 3,000ha of tropical savanna

children.content: The federal environment minister's decision to wave through plans to bulldoze almost 3,000 hectares — an area 10 times the size of Sydney's CBD — of habitat, home to threatened species, will be challenged in the Federal Court.

image.altText: Murray Watt frowns.

items.title: NT child protection bill one year in the making had 'limited' consultation

children.content: Despite taking a year to prepare a controversial bill surrounding child protection, the NT Department of Children and Families says consultation with organisations was "limited".

image.altText: Front-on shot of white man, pale blue shirt, and bright blue striped tie, sitting at long white desk, inside high-rise office

items.title: Police say cause of Kumanjayi White's death in custody still not known

children.content: The Northern Territory police commissioner says the cause of 24-year-old Kumanjayi White's death in custody remains undetermined, with the criminal case against the officers who restrained him now closed.

articles.title: Kumanjayi White family 'heartbroken' no charges laid over custody death

children.content: The family of Kumanjayi White tell the ABC they are "heartbroken" that no charges will be laid over his death in custody, as Northern Territory Police confirm their criminal investigation has concluded.

image.altText: A group of Aboriginal mourners walking through a coles supermarket, while holding branches.

gallery.altText: A woman bends over in mourning next to a pile of flowers laid on the ground.

thumbnail.altText: A woman bends over in mourning next to a pile of flowers laid on the ground.

items.title: Feds warn NT's law-and-order agenda could breach human rights

children.content: Federal Attorney-General Michelle Rowland warns her Northern Territory counterpart that the NT's tough-on-crime agenda could breach Australia's international human rights obligations.

image.altText: Rowland stands in the prime minister's courtyard smiling.

items.title: Brother of hit-run victim says family will appeal NT court's 'light' sentence

children.content: The family of a man who was left to die alongside his severely injured brother and father after a hit-and-run crash, say they will appeal the "light" sentence handed to the man who was behind the wheel.

image.altText: A coat of arms on a building, alongside the words: Supreme Court.

items.title: NT paramedics consider body-worn cameras amid ongoing violent assaults

children.content: The Northern Territory's ambulance service is considering introducing police-style body-worn cameras after the arrest of alleged murderer Jefferson Lewis led to rioting in Alice Springs last month.

gallery.altText: Two paramedics in uniform pose with an ambulance

thumbnail.altText: Two paramedics in uniform pose with an ambulance

items.title: 'About bloody time': BOM selects new NT radar site 11 years after closure

children.content: BOM says it has decided to install a new weather radar in Tennant Creek, in the same place as the previous radar.

image.altText: Tennant Creek radar

items.title: Man's death confirmed as diphtheria-related after overseas toxicology test

children.content: The Northern Territory government says the rate of new diphtheria cases in the territory has fallen from more than 20 per week to less than 10 after 10,000 Territorians stepped up to be immunised against the ongoing outbreak.

image.altText: Steve Edgington wears a suit and tie while standing outside NT Parliament.

items.title: Budget's negative gearing, CGT legislation to be introduced to parliament

children.content: The federal government will introduce legislation to parliament today to overhaul negative gearing and the capital gains tax. Follow live.

image.altText: Albanese is in focus as he sits on the green bench watching Chalmers speak

items.title: Young saviour and an answered prayer deliver Blues incredible Origin win

children.content: When the Blues needed heroes to get on top of the Maroons in State of Origin I, they turned to a debutante and the oldest player on the field.

image.altText: Ethan Strange celebrates a Blues try in State of Origin I.

items.title: Israel warns all residents in southern Lebanon to leave

children.content: It is the first time Israel has made such a sweeping demand since US President Donald Trump announced in April a ceasefire had been imposed in Lebanon.

image.altText: A large smoke cloud billows from behind residential buildings.

items.title: UK's social media ban could be harsher than Australia's. Here's how it compares

children.content: The UK could become the latest nation to implement a social media ban, after British Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer vowed to introduce a "game changer" policy.

image.altText: Keir Starmer in a dark shirt with his sleeves rolled up, siting while smiling next to two children with red hats.

items.title: Brothers torn apart by Stolen Generations policies share their story

children.content: Iain Seymour never knew he was Aboriginal until finding out he was adopted three years ago and then meeting his brother Allen Edwards, the son of a woman who played a key part in setting up a poignant memorial to the long-term effects of the Stolen Generations.
Warning: This story contains images of Indigenous people who have died.

thumbnail.altText: Two men with arms on each other's shoulders looking at a fire

items.title: Divers begin complex mission to rescue men found alive in flooded Laos cave

children.content: Five of the seven people, who entered the cave in central Laos more than a week ago, have been found alive. Rescuers are still searching for two.

image.altText: Group of villagers trapped inside Laos cave in the dark looking relieved to be found.

items.title: Iran's internet is back on. But it's not what it used to be

children.content: Reports of an increase in internet traffic are filtering through, but experts say Iran has a long way to go to get back up to speed.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a man's hands typing on a keyboard in a dark room.

items.title: Why this photo has been terrifying the internet for years

children.content: Forget about slashers and body horror — the next evolution of fright stems from an fluorescent-lit room.

items.title: Melbourne teacher goes missing in India

children.content: The family of maths teacher Sunil Sharma say the 66-year-old disappeared nearly a week ago, while trying to sell a property in the Punjabi city of Amritsar.

image.altText: Surbhi and Sunil Sharma smile, taking a selfie together in a city.

items.title: Torquay wagtails increasing in numbers around the world

children.content: A Victorian artist paints hundreds of willie wagtails a week to give away locally and now they're popping up all over the world.

image.altText: Mike McLean paints his willie wagtails on pieces of reclaimed timber.

items.title: Quiz: Can you identify Aussie TV shows from a single frame?

children.content: How well do you know your Neighbours from your Deadloch? Take our single-frame Australian TV quiz to find out.

image.altText: Show artwork for Kath & Kim, featuring Kath and Kim standing side by side in bold outfits and hats, holding glasses of wine

items.title: As the debate heats up, migrants are quietly keeping the bush running

children.content: Amid ongoing discussion about Australia's immigration intake and migration policies, regional residents say many industries in the bush cannot survive without overseas workers.

image.altText: workers from the pacific in red dirt field planting trees with tractor in front of them

items.title: Can farmers become less reliant on synthetic fertiliser? Some are trying

children.content: A war thousands of kilometres away from Australia's farms is quietly reshaping what crops are planted and what will be available at supermarkets in the future.

gallery.altText: Man stands for portrait among apple orchard

thumbnail.altText: Man stands for portrait among apple orchard

items.title: Modelling suggests 'second wave' of algal bloom in Spencer Gulf

children.content: Modelling predicts that the algal bloom that has plagued South Australia's coastlines since March 2025 will be moving further through the Spencer Gulf in the next few months.

image.altText: A man sits at a table near the beach with a laptop.

items.title: 'To embarrass me?': Apology demanded over leak to media of personal information

children.content: A Tasmanian independent politician is calling for an apology after her personal health-related request via email to the government was leaked to a media outlet.

image.altText: Ruth Forrest, Tasmanian Legislative Council _ Upper House 2025-09-23 10:09:00

items.title: Bike shop burns down, factory torched on fiery night in Melbourne

children.content: Firefighters responded to separate blazes in Melbourne overnight, with police investigating one as an arson attack.

image.altText: A firefighter stands at the top of a high ladder aiming water at the roof of a building where flames can be seen at night.

items.title: Trump says US 'not satisfied' with Iran peace deal proposals

children.content: It comes as the White House rejects a draft deal being reported by Iranian state TV as a "fabrication".

image.altText: An oil tanker docked with multiple vessels around it next to a crude oil terminal.

items.title: PM response to truth-telling process 'hugely significant'

children.content: The Walk for Truth ended at Parliament House today and while there was not a firm commitment from the PM, many felt the response was a 'positive move'.

gallery.altText: Anthony albanese shaking Travis lovett hands

thumbnail.altText: Anthony albanese shaking Travis lovett hands

items.title: Matthew Perry's assistant jailed for role in Friends actor's death

children.content: Matthew Perry's live-in personal assistant, who had a central role in the Friends star's descent into ketamine addiction and injected him with the fatal dose of the drug, has been sentenced to three years and five months in prison.

image.altText: A picture of Matthew Perry at a film premiere.

items.title: UK spy chief says 500,000 Russians killed in Ukraine war so far

children.content: The head of a major UK intelligence agency is warning that Moscow and other adversaries could use artificial intelligence and other emerging technology to conduct "hybrid warfare" against western allies.

image.altText: A man stands in front of the Russian flag.

items.title: FIFA faces investigation over 'impossibly high' World Cup ticket prices

children.content: In a joint statement, New Jersey's attorney-general accused the football governing body of having turned "buying a ticket to the World Cup into a gauntlet of confusion, fake scarcity, and impossibly high prices".

image.altText: The FIFA World Cup 2026 logo on the side of a stadium roof.

items.title: ISIS-linked woman barred from Australia tried to board flight

children.content: An Australian woman with links to ISIS, who is barred from Australia on a temporary exclusion order, tried to board a flight home with the other members of Tuesday's returning cohort, a Senate estimates hearing has been told.

image.altText: A Qatar plane on the tarmac at Melbourne international airport.

items.title: Kalshi and Polymarket are billion dollar bets banned from Australia — for now

children.content: Kalshi and Polymarket have gone from obscure start-ups to two of the world's biggest prediction markets where users bet on yes-or-no questions spanning politics, pop culture and war.

items.title: Labor MP defends electoral registration to empty block amid rebuild

children.content: Dickson MP Ali France has been referred to the Australian Electoral Commission with claims she is registered to vote at a vacant suburban block in her north Brisbane electorate.

items.title: A pipe bursts every 73 minutes in Qld, report warns situation set to worsen

children.content: Queensland councils are calling for more state funding to update the state's pipe network, which they say has deteriorated to a high-risk condition.

image.altText: vlc water main

items.title: This 24yo started gambling as a teenager and has now lost 900k

children.content: From footy tips to Taylor Swift bets, teenagers are being lured into gambling. Almost 34 per cent of Australian teenagers gamble and everyone from teachers, experts, to the young people themselves are sounding the alarm.
